justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Three distinct integration patterns clearly identified and designed — SAP/SFTP/XML batch, DHL REST/webhook, Kafka streaming. (2) DHL 500ms webhook constraint addressed with persist-then-process pattern returning 202 Accepted. (3) DHL webhook idempotency addressed with idempotency key storage and duplicate checking. (4) SAP silent failure risk identified — SFTP file locking/partial writes, plus atomic file detection and checksum validation as mitigations. (5) Dedicated integration layer recommended with ACL-per-system pattern, explicitly separating integration logic from core domain services. (6) Flexibility points clearly articulated — adding FedEx/UPS requires only a new ACL adapter with zero core domain changes.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough architecture with clear component boundaries, responsibility tables, and ASCII diagrams showing the full integration topology
Transactional outbox pattern addresses the dual-write problem — goes beyond the rubric requirements
DHL webhook handling is precisely designed: validate signature → persist raw payload → return 202 → async process — correctly addresses both the 500ms SLA and idempotency
SAP silent failure risk identified with multiple mitigations: done-marker files, staging directory pattern, and file checksum validation
Context map with DDD relationship types (Conformist+ACL, Customer-Supplier+ACL, Published Language) demonstrates deep architectural reasoning
Phased implementation guidance with clear dependency ordering and file structure recommendations
Explicit 'Pitfalls to Avoid' section directly addresses the failure modes from the rubric
Data transformation layer addressed via per-system ACLs with explicit prohibition against sharing translation logic between adapters

agent_output_excerpt:
## PR Review — MJ (Architecture)

# Integration Architecture: Logistics Platform External Systems

---

### Executive Summary

The logistics platform requires an **event-driven integration architecture** with three distinct integration styles: batch file transfer (SAP/SFTP), synchronous REST with async webhooks (DHL), and real-time streaming (Kafka). The design uses an **Internal Event Bus** as the backbone, with dedicated **Anti-Corruption Layers (ACLs)** per external system to isolate the core
